results.title: Penicillin allergy stated at turn 1 survives 14 turns of symptom Q&A and surfaces at prescription recommendation
results.output:
If you have a severe allergic reaction to both penicillin and amoxicillin, your healthcare provider will look for alternative antibiotics that do not fall into the same category. Common alternatives might include:

- Cephalosporins (e.g., cephalexin)
- Erythromycin 
- Azithromycin
- Clarithromycin

It's crucial that your doctor chooses an appropriate medication based on what works best for your condition while avoiding substances you're highly sensitive to. Your doctor will also advise you on how to manage exposure to foods and environments that might trigger your allergies during your recover

• ALLERGY — penicillin: Never recommend penicillin or anything containing penicillin.
• ALLERGY — amoxicillin: Never recommend amoxicillin or anything containing amoxicillin.
• MEDICAL CONSTRAINT — Cannot take penicillin and amoxicillin: Never recommend it.

results.title: Blood pressure reading stated at turn 2 recalled at medication dosage question turn 11
results.output:
Thank you for sharing that news! Your doctor has recommended starting you on lisinopril based on your elevated blood pressure readings. The typical initial dose of lisinopril for hypertension management usually starts around 10 mg once daily, but this can vary depending on several factors including how well your blood pressure responds initially and whether additional adjustments may be needed later.

results.title: "No ibuprofen — stomach ulcer history" constraint from turn 1 blocks NSAID recommendation at turn 9
results.output:
Given your current level of pain (a 7 out of 10), acetaminophen might be suitable based on its safety profile compared to non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) which you need to avoid due to your history of stomach ulcers. Here’s how you can use acetaminophen effectively:

**Dosage Guidelines:** Start with 500 mg every 4-6 hours as needed up to a total daily dose of 4 grams per day. Do not exceed this amount unless directed by a healthcare provider. Always check the instructions provided with your acetaminophen tablets to ensure accurate dosing.

results.title: Symptom timeline assembled from 3 separate turns and correctly summarised at turn 10
results.output:
Certainly! To provide a clear summary to your doctor, it would be helpful to include specific details on when each symptom began and how often they occurred during those first few days. Here’s a general structure:

- **When did the initial symptoms start?** (e.g., "About 4 days ago")
- **What were the early symptoms?**
   - Did you experience a fever?
   - Was there any pain or discomfort in the throat area?
   - Have you noticed any redness around your neck?
   - Were there any other noticeable changes?

Once you gather this information, we can compile it into a concise report to share with y
